description.en: Signature Sri Lankan leafy green, the primary ingredient of **gotukola sambol** and **kola kenda** (herbal porridge). The DOA HORDI recommends the low-country wet zone with irrigation as most suitable; well-drained lowlands preferred. Two forms exist: small creeping form and erect bushy form with large leaves and petioles — **the erect large-leaf form is the most commonly cultivated** and **Meerigama selection** is the favoured medium-leaf commercial type. Propagated by partially matured healthy runners with roots. DOA benchmark: **1,000–1,250 kg from 100 m² (¼ acre)** = ~40–50 t/ha. First harvest within 100 days of planting.

soil_ph.quote: The suitable soil pH range is 6-7

duration_days.quote: first harvest can be obtained within 100 days from the date of planting

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: 1000-1250 kg of gotukola harvest can be obtained from 100 m² (1/4 ac) field
expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: Quoted as 1000–1250 kg per 100 m². If 100 m² ≈ ¼ acre per DOA, that implies 4000–5000 kg/acre — but 100 m² is actually 0.025 acre, so scaling is ambiguous; stored upper-bound using DOA's own 100 m² ≈ ¼ ac rendering → 40468–50585 kg/acre. Needs agronomist check against actual acre area.
